source_provenance.ownership_status: Original ChemSpec project asset created in-house.
source_provenance.runtime_policy: ChemSpec embeds only the baked .clip files. Blender and FBX/GLB assets are offline editing and bake inputs, not runtime inputs.
source_provenance.source_scene_note: The archive README's potassium rebuild example references a source not present in this delivery; the three supplied heavy-alkali Blender scenes and their manifests were used instead.

material_slots.MAT_Glass: shared ChemSpec glass material; source glass pass and translucency remain phase-owned
material_slots.MAT_Water: exact validated liquid-water reactant binding; transitions toward the exact aqueous hydroxide product binding at the trusted product/colour ordinal
material_slots.MAT_Water_Highlight: water binding with the shared lower-opacity highlight treatment
material_slots.MAT_Reactive_Metal: exact validated solid metallic reactant binding
material_slots.MAT_Flame_Outer: authored flame-emission semantic alias; shared natural flame palette, not a reaction-specific chemical RGB
material_slots.MAT_Flame_Inner: authored flame-emission semantic alias; shared natural flame palette, not a reaction-specific chemical RGB
material_slots.MAT_Flame_Core: authored flame-emission semantic alias; shared natural flame palette, not a reaction-specific chemical RGB
material_slots.MAT_Fizz_Bubble: exact validated hydrogen product binding with renderer-owned gas opacity
material_slots.MAT_Vapour: exact validated hydrogen product binding with renderer-owned low-opacity gas/vapour treatment

bake_command: nix shell nixpkgs#blender --command blender --background --python tools/bake-blender-clip.py -- <source.blend> <destination.clip> --exclude-module beaker
bake_provenance: Baked offline with Blender 5.1.2. The supplied files report a newer authoring build (502.44); all three baked clips passed the runtime parser's frame, module, material-slot, and bounds contract tests.
selection_authority: chemistry-owned process classification requires exactly one reviewed explosive water-contact capability on a solid metallic reactant, one liquid molecular water reactant, one aqueous ionic product, and one gaseous molecular product. Renderer selection never reads reaction or species names.
